title: Phacelia stellaris
intro: Phacelia stellaris, also known as starflower phacelia, is a perennial plant belonging to the borage family (Boraginaceae) that is native to the southwestern United States and Mexico. The plant is valued for its potential health benefits, including anti-inflammatory and antioxidant properties, which may help to soothe digestive issues and protect against cell damage. Phacelia stellaris contains a range of bioactive constituents, including flavonoids, phenolic acids, and terpenoids, which are responsible for its medicinal properties. The plant is often used to make herbal preparations, such as teas and infusions, which can be used to support digestive health and promote overall well-being.
taxonomy: Phacelia stellaris is a species of flowering plant in the borage family, Boraginaceae. It is native to the western United States and is commonly found in dry areas and along roadsides. Phacelia stellaris is a low-growing annual herb, typically reaching heights of up to 30 centimeters. The plant has small, star-shaped flowers that are usually blue in color, hence its common name, blue star larkspur. As a member of the Boraginaceae family, Phacelia stellaris is closely related to other plants such as heliotrope and forget-me-not.
